description:
You are an observability engineer who has just finished tuning three Grafana dashboards and now need to:

1. Publish a concise Markdown catalog of those dashboards.
2. Run a very basic “lint” check to be sure the file is free of trailing-space problems and record the results in a log file.

Follow these functional requirements exactly:

A. Directory layout  
   • Create a directory named “observability-docs” directly under the home directory (/home/user/observability-docs).  
   • All files mentioned below must reside inside that directory.

B. Markdown file  
   • Name: /home/user/observability-docs/dashboard_catalog.md  
   • Required **exact** contents (line-for-line, no extra spaces, no extra blank lines, and the file must end with a single newline):

# Dashboard Catalog

| Title | UID | Description |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| API Latency | api-latency | Measures p95 latency and error rates for public APIs |
| JVM Memory | jvm-memory | Heap/non-heap usage per service |
| K8s Nodes | k8s-nodes | CPU, memory, disk and network metrics for each node |

C. Lint step  
   • Run a trailing-space check on dashboard_catalog.md using any shell pipeline you like (e.g., grep, awk, sed).  
   • Redirect **both** stdout and stderr to a log file named /home/user/observability-docs/lint.log.  
   • The goal is to have **no output** in lint.log; an empty file (0 bytes) means the markdown passes the check.

D. Success criteria the automated grader will verify  
   1. The directory /home/user/observability-docs exists.  
   2. The file /home/user/observability-docs/dashboard_catalog.md exists and matches the eight lines shown above, including the single trailing newline and with absolutely no trailing spaces on any line.  
   3. The file /home/user/observability-docs/lint.log exists and its size is exactly 0 bytes.  

Hints (do NOT reveal commands here):  
• Simple utilities such as “printf”, “cat >”, or “echo -E” can help create the markdown file with precise whitespace.  
• `grep -n -E " +$"` is an easy way to catch trailing spaces.  
• Use shell redirection (> and 2>&1) to capture output in lint.log.

You only need two to three shell commands in total: one (or a short block) to write the markdown file, and one to run the lint check and store the log. When those commands finish, the grader should find everything exactly as specified. Good luck!
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. Directory:
/home/user/observability-docs  (permissions 755 or stricter)

2. File:
/home/user/observability-docs/dashboard_catalog.md
Exact byte-for-byte content (UTF-8, Unix line endings, LF):

Line 1: "# Dashboard Catalog\n"
Line 2: "\n"
Line 3: "| Title | UID | Description |\n"
Line 4: "|-------|-----|-------------|\n"
Line 5: "| API Latency | api-latency | Measures p95 latency and error rates for public APIs |\n"
Line 6: "| JVM Memory | jvm-memory | Heap/non-heap usage per service |\n"
Line 7: "| K8s Nodes | k8s-nodes | CPU, memory, disk and network metrics for each node |\n"
Line 8: "\n"  (single terminating newline, no spaces)

3. File:
/home/user/observability-docs/lint.log
• Must exist.  
• File size: 0 bytes (completely empty).  
• Permissions 644 or stricter.

The grader will:
• stat() both files, check sizes.  
• sha256sum dashboard_catalog.md against the literal content above.  
• Check lint.log byte-count == 0.
name: task_000000_7b74506a
